title: MessageCreateInput.sent_at is &str not UTCDate — validation footgun
description: MessageCreateInput.sent_at is typed as &str even though UTCDate::parse() now exists. A caller can pass any string — malformed or non-UTC — and it serializes silently into the sentAt JSON field. UTCDate::new() validates non-emptiness; UTCDate::parse() validates RFC 3339 format. Using &str here bypasses both. The fix is to type the field as UTCDate (or &UTCDate) so that callers are guided to the typed API and format errors surface at construction rather than at server rejection. Until changed, every new caller of message_create is a potential interop bug.
